data.narrative:
Deep Sentinel is a Series B-stage defense and security company that has raised $55M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms—with primary focus on 6 same-sector, same-stage private peers including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $55M total raised as a baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding an implied value of $104M. Peer-median analysis across the 6 same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $1.2B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B. This peer-based method reflects market pricing for established defense-tech companies at similar maturity.

The combined analysis supports a valuation range of $89M to $1.4B, with a median of $652M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty: the stage-progression method anchors conservatively to capital deployed, while peer benchmarking suggests substantially higher valuations if Deep Sentinel is operationally tracking leading comparables. Resolution of this gap hinges on revenue scale, customer concentration, and technical differentiation relative to the peer set. Additional data on ARR, customer count, and gross margin would narrow the range materially.
